guidelinesCompliance.evidence: All documentation emphasizes AI Cockpit works with any agent (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, etc.) and does not prescribe specific workflows.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Emphasize governance stability over workflow evolution
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Roadmap and README clearly state 'Agents evolve continuously. Governance should remain stable.'

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Intent fields remain optional—do not make them mandatory
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: All documentation consistently describes intent fields as optional. Roadmap states 'all fields optional' design decision.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Maintain backward compatibility with V1 Contract format
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: No schema changes made. Documentation-only work item. V2 schema already exists and is backward-compatible.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Focus on 'what should change' rather than prescribing implementation details
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Documentation describes governance concepts and principles without prescribing agent implementation details.

risk.detail: Documentation-only changes. No code or schema modifications. All changes maintain backward compatibility.

reviewReadiness.reason: All acceptance criteria met. Documentation accurately reflects Intent → Contract → Implementation → Verification → Summary governance loop. Four design principles stated clearly. V2 implementation plan created.
expectedReviewFocus: Verify new architectural positioning accurately reflects project vision
Confirm design principles (Model Agnostic, Stable Schema, Governance over Workflow, Intent-driven Development) are clear and consistent
Review governance loop visualization and terminology across all documentation
Ensure backward compatibility messaging is preserved

checkpointEvidence.reason: Contract complete with scope, intent, and acceptance criteria defined
All documentation changes complete, summary updated with intent alignment

overclaimPrevention: Documentation changes only. No code changes, no schema changes, no behavioral changes to verify. Quality checks will validate documentation consistency.

intentAlignment.problemResolutionEvidence: AI Cockpit architectural positioning has been clarified from mixed 'collaborative environment' messaging to clear 'Repository Governance Layer' positioning. V2 intent schema is now positioned as first-class governance object. Complete governance loop documented: Intent → Contract → Implementation → Verification → Summary (Intent Alignment).
intentAlignment.constraintsRespectEvidence: No code changes (documentation only). No schema changes (V2 already stable). Backward compatibility maintained. No workflow breaking changes. All intent fields remain optional.
intentAlignment.rationaleValidated: Realignment establishes clear foundation for V2 adoption and natural extensibility to V3/V4. By positioning Intent as first-class and AI Cockpit as governance (not runtime), we create logical progression: V3 Repository Intelligence and V4 Organization Governance extend the Intent → Execution → Validation → Alignment loop naturally.
